name: Southwestern Corn and Potato Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 small Fresh Chile, seeded and minced
1 small Red bell pepper, finely chopped
3 cup Fresh or frozen corn kernels
recipeInstructions:
1. In a covered soup pot, simmer onions, garlic, chile, and salt in 1 cup of vegetable broth for about 10 minutes, or until onions are soft.
2. In a small bowl, make a paste with cumin and 1 tablespoon of broth, stirring it into the pot. Simmer for another 1 to 2 minutes.
3. Add sweet potato and remaining broth, simmering for 10 minutes until sweet potato softens.
4. Add bell pepper and corn, simmering, covered, for another 10 minutes until all vegetables are tender.
5. Puree about half of the soup in a blender or food processor and return it to the pot. The soup will be creamy and thick.
6. If desired, add another cup of vegetable broth plus salt to taste. Gently reheat on low heat.
7. Serve with a dollop of reduced-fat sour cream and chopped cilantro, if desired. Makes 4 servings.
